string.text: Do I need a test case?
Here's a test case for beta.
I will find it.
Don't.
Good
I completed the quest
Excellent.
Where is my reward?
There she is!
Thank you.
Thank you.
Do you know where to get some work?

Yes, a fresh rumour came in just this morning. A new group of researchers has arrived in the Zone, collecting strange artefacts. They're not even real artefacts, more like irradiated rocks, but for some reason they can't get enough. They might need a hand.
That sounds interesting. Where can I find them?
Eggheads never pay enough. Forget it.
They're scattered all over the Zone, but there's one nearby who's in the Great Swamps. He seems to have set up camp in the Old Church.
Hello Professor, may I ask how your research is going?
Ah, another assistant! I have a matter I need some help with. It should be easy for someone like you.
Sure, I could take the job. What do you need?
Never mind, I don't have time for this.

I require samples from the local anomalous fields for my experiments. You need to go into these anomalies and find almost-formed artefacts. Your detector should be able to pick them up once you're close to them.
Sounds easy, I'll get you your special rocks.
I would like to help, but I don't have an artefact detector. Do you have one I could borrow?
Very good. I will mark the anomalies I want you to search on your PDA.

Well, I suppose I can give you my old detector. It's outdated, but it should do the trick. If you do a couple of these jobs for us, you will be able to buy a better one in no time! Check your PDA for the locations I want you to investigate.
About your mission...
How goes the search? Have you found my samples yet?
No, I am still looking for them.
I didn't find anything actually.
Are you certain? My calculations clearly prove that you should find something there. I'm sure you wouldn't want to block the progress of science.
I'm sorry Prof, but I couldn't find them.
Alright. If you're sure, I'll keep looking.
Damn it, you're serious. This is not good, it'll push my timetable back by months... Sorry, I have to get back to the lab. Goodbye, assistant.
Hello professor, may I ask how your research is going?.
Don't forget to use the detector I gave you, it should make a beeping sound when you are close to an artefact.
Here's your sample. Where's my reward?
Oh, of course, here is your research stipend. And a little bonus, though helping science is its own reward.
Anything else you need help with?

Yes, actually. Previously, I was studying the anomalies at Agroprom and found a number of strange readings. Artefacts that form in these anomalies have unique characteristics from noospheric interference - really fascinating stuff. However, during our field test, we were attacked and had to flee. In the confusion, I left my laptop behind - it has all our anomaly field scanning data. I need you to find my laptop, and look for artefact samples in the anomalous fields that it mentions.
Hey Prof, I found your laptop and got the artefact samples.
Incredible, I thought I was never going to get them! I hope this reward is enough for all the trouble you went through.
I'm listening.
Why don't you ask one of your colleagues from Yantar?

Don't lump me together with those mediocre amateurs at Yantar. Those idiots haven't even bothered to study the anomalies on their front door. They can barely tell the difference between a rock and a proto-artefact. Listen, I'm much more qualified. The sooner you find me the samples, the better.
Here are your samples, as requested.
Yes, yes, thank you, assistant. Now, where will I put these...
Anything else you need help with, Professor?

While I was out on a field study in the Wild Territory, I took some promising readings from the anomalous fields there. Samples from those fields could confirm my theories about noospheric factors in artefact formation. I'll give you the coordinates of the areas I want you to sample.
My hair is still standing up from static shock, after dodging those electro anomalies. I hope you appreciate this batch of samples.
Nicely done, assistant. Your reward, as promised.
Anything else? I was hoping for something more... exciting.

Exciting, eh? I've got a risky field study waiting for an ambitious assistant. Just north of here, in Yantar, there is an old laboratory which emits dangerous levels of psy emissions. It's the perfect place to find samples to prove that psy energy affects artefact formation. You've proven yourself trustworthy, and I'm sure you are capable for this task. You should talk to Professor Sakharov if you need some sort of psy protection.
That definitely wasn't easy. I think I got all your samples.
Astonishing! You really are a very capable assistant.
You seem troubled.

Oh, do I? This shithole is filled with mutants and bandits waiting to rip me a new one. Those idiot professors sent me here by myself without any help, just because I'm a candidate and not a professor! Did they think this was a simple walk in the park? Sakharov has a lot to answer for!
Woah, calm down man. Maybe I could help you. What do you need?

If you're serious about helping me, there are some rare anomalies that have been overlooked by the bandits. If you could fight them off and get me a sample from each one, I would be very grateful. Here are the coordinates.
That was one hell of a fight, but I managed to get your samples.
Finally, some good news. I'll let Sakharov know right away. Thank you stalker, you really saved my ass.
Anything else?

Unfortunately, yes. Sakharov wants me to check out the Truck Cemetery. He says there are some "anomalies of interest" there, but that place is poorly explored for good reasons. If you get me samples from these anomalies, I'll give you a quarter - no, half of what they're paying me for this trip. The area is just north of here. Watch out for the bandits and mutants, my friend.
Man, that place is a dump. Here's your next batch of samples, professor.
Hah, I'm not a professor yet! As promised, half of my stipend. You do fine work.
So, anything else or was that it?

Well, there is one more thing. The scientists sent a fellow candidate to Garbage, to place some anomaly measurement prototypes in the area. But he's missed his last two radio check-ins, and I'm afraid something might have happened to him.
That wouldn't be surprising in this irradiated shithole.

The last time we talked, he said he was going to stock up at the Flea Market, so I would start looking there. Find him and his PDA, it should contain the locations of the remaining anomalies we were supposed to study. Please, if you can find him and his samples, we can finally leave this godforsaken place.
I found your fellow student. Seems he was jumped and killed by bandits, but I managed to recover his PDA and finish the job. Here you go, Pirogov.

What a rotten way to die... Shot in the back over some samples they couldn't bother to collect themselves. As promised, here's your reward. Take care of yourself, stalker. I can't wait to leave this hellhole and get back to the bunker.
Hey, I heard you researchers are looking for help.
A little eager, hmm? Are you trying to take credit for my work?
Not at all, Professor. I'm in it for the money.

In it for the gold, not the glory? That suits me very well. This area is full of fascinating anomalies that you stalkers mostly avoid. Get me samples from the ones I marked on your PDA, and then we can talk further.
Here you go, one batch of special rocks.
Great. As promised, here's your payment. Now, what else can I help you with?
How do you even know where these artefacts will appear?

That is what we at the Scientific Institute for Research of the Chernobyl Anomalous Area, or SIRCAA, are here to do. Using our own preliminary anomaly maps created through our expeditions, data from you locals, and experimental theoretical algorithms, we have created a predictive model for artefact formation. Simply put: we can predict exactly where new artefacts are about to form.
Interesting. So that's how you know where to send me all the time.

Precisely. Now, I have another task for you, but this one requires caution. You seem more capable than the average stalker, and I would hate to see our relationship end so quickly. I tasked another stalker with investigating some anomalous fields in the Red Forest. Unfortunately, he seems to have gone missing. The last time he messaged me was from was the collapsed tunnel, at the bridge to Limansk. Go and find his PDA, which contains the scan data I gave him, and find the remaining samples. I must warn you however, I have lost many assistants to the Red Forest. I hope you won't be one of them.
The Red Forest is as dangerous as you said. I barely made it out with your samples - the reward had better cover my hazard pay.
Wonderful, you really are as capable as I had hoped. Here's your reward, and I think you'll find it quite agreeable.

All these artefact samples you've sent me to collect are about how artefacts are made - are you trying to make man-made artefacts? The need for the Zone would fall dramatically, if they could be created in a lab. Is this what SIRCAA is trying to do?

Clever man. It goes without saying that this stays between you and I. Yes, I have worked on the creation of artificial artefacts even before the Zone existed. My supervisor used to live and work in Limansk. We spent many long hours pushing the boundaries of science, to figure out the origins of artefacts. After appropriating a grow bed from the lab, we even managed to create a proto-artefact, just like the ones you've brought me.
Interesting. What happened to your supervisor?

I don't know. There was no evacuation from Limansk at the time of the disaster. After all these years, I never found out what happened to Limansk's inhabitatnts. But... I have a foolish hope that there might still be something at his old house. If I give you the address to his house, could you go there and see if the artefact we made is still there? Who knows, maybe it is the key to understanding all of this.
Limansk is in ruins. The only people there are insane fanatics. I have some good news, though. I found that artefact you made. Here it is.
You managed to fight through Limansk? I knew the chances were slim but this is more than I dreamed of finding. Old friend, I will honor your sacrifice. I will find the origin of artefacts.
How's it going, Prof? Any news on the origin of artefacts?

Yes, I have great news. This artefact has a completely unique emission pattern. Do you know what this means? My friend, right here, we have indisputable proof that artefacts can be created by artificial means.
That's crazy! Wait, are you telling me it's possible that artefacts could have appeared before the Zone did?

You have practically put it together yourself. Yes, research into the creation of artefacts existed before the disaster happened. All such experiments happened in the X-19 laboratory, before it was abandoned. I mentioned getting a grow bed before - that's where we got it from. These grow beds allowed artefacts to form in controlled conditions. Nowadays, that lab is overrun by fanatics, who call themselves Monolith. Needless to say, it is extremely dangerous. If you can somehow get there and bring back leftover artefacts or documents, you would help humanity enter a new scientific era.
You aren't going to believe it. I managed to fight through the swarms of Monolith in X-19, and you were right. I found three artifical artefacts and some papers that looked important. Here you go.

As always, stalker, you exceed my expectations. And I'm sure you will be discreet, as you can understand that we must perfect this process before we reveal it to the world. There's just a few final things I need before I can publish my work to the world.
My lips are sealed, Professor. What is it that you need?

We have received some information that the Dead City area - yes, the one controlled by mercenaries - contains unusually stable anomaly resonances. One final round of samples from Dead City would be irrefutable proof for my artefact formation theory.
The city didn't seem all that "dead" to me. Here are the samples, prof.

Splendid work, here is your reward. With how much you have helped me, you might merit a mention in the acknowledgements of my paper. If you want more work, you might want to look for some of my colleagues, here in the Zone. Maybe they will have something for you. I'll tell them to expect you.
Hello, I was told you might have some work for me.

I do, actually. There are a few anomalous fields I want to study, but I have to oversee some scanner data collection here and can't leave. Sakharov will be pissed if I can't send any results to him. I'll mark the anomalies of interest in on your PDA. Let me know if you find anything there.
I checked out those anomalies. Here's some samples from them.
Huh, I wasn't sure there was going to be anything there. Good work, here is your reward.
Is there anything else I can help you with?

While you were out in the field, my data collection finally finished. As you well know, plenty of anomalies have not yet been mapped, but my project is trying to fix this. I'll send you some of my preliminary scan data, with the approximate location of some anomaly fields. Please confirm if the data is right, and keep an eye out for any artefacts. Be careful, the location data isn't precise yet.
I used up most of my bolts proving you correct. I also managed to find some artefacts that you might want to look at.
Great, this confirms our scanners are working fine. Thank you for your report, the money should be with you shortly.
Got any more work for me?

As it happens, yes I do. You see, there are these extremely interesting emission patterns in Pripyat. But that place is as dangerous as it is fascinating, and I wouldn't stand a chance in there. Can you go there, investigate these locations and bring me any artefacts you can find? Don't worry, the reward will more than cover your costs for ammo and medicine.
You're lucky I love science. I think I still have a bullet lodged up my asshole from the fighting in Pripyat. Here's your damn samples and your damn data.

Phenomenal! Please take this reward, you have more than earned it. That's everything I needed to do out here in the Zone. If you want to earn some more money collecting artefacts, you might want to search for some of my colleagues in the Zone. I'll let them know you're coming.
Hey, do you have a job for me?

I might just. Yesterday, in order to pinpoint anomalous fields for investigation, we restored some of the equipment at the Krug Antenna Complex. However, our scans took too long to complete, and we had to leave for our own safety before the scans were complete. I'll pay you to enter the station and bring back the memory module I left there. All the necessary data should be on it. While you're there, can you check out the nearby anomalies? The data on that module should tell you which ones to search for samples.
Not a problem for a stalker like me. I've got your module, your scans, and your samples. Give me my reward and it's all yours.
Good news for a change! Here's the money, as we agreed. Hmm, this scanner data is quite curious...
What is it, Professor?

Some of these readings are contradicting each other, it makes no sense. Could you go check them out in person? I'm sure you know the drill by now, bring back any promising samples and I'll compensate you for your time.
I checked the locations you mentioned. And, yes, here are the samples.
Such excellent specimens. I'm making great progress, thanks to you. Here is your reward.
Got any more paying work?

This is all for now. If you want to help us further, then I will send you to a colleague of mine, who would appreciate your help. I'll send you his location and let him know you are coming. Farewell stalker!

I need to analyze our existing data, so I don't need your help for now, but you should move north to the Chernobyl Nuclear Power Plant. The head of our research group, Professor Skabinsky, is already there. I have no idea how he got there so fast, but it can't be safe. Please, hurry north for his sake! He controls our grant money, so I'm sure he can reward you.

We don't have the field data we need to finish our calculations, but we have a rough location for some very promising anomalous fields around the Chernobyl Nuclear Power Plant. The head of our research group, Professor Skabinsky, went to examine it. It's dangerous there and I don't know if he has any backup. You're a capable and proven stalker. Please, hurry north - he needs help, even if he won't admit it. And I'm sure he'll pay well.
Hello, stalker.
Hello.
Hello, brother.
What the hell is a professor like you doing out here? How did you even make it here, past the Monolith fanatics?

Oh, you must be the helpful stalker Vorobushkin mentioned. I paid a group of mercenaries escort me here, but most of them got shot on the way up. No matter, a uniquely dangerous anomaly is nearby. If my calculations are correct, and they often are, a very rare and powerful artefact is about to form. You have a reputation for being quite the artefact hunter. We need you to head in there and see if our predictions are correct. Are you up for it?
I've come all this way, and I'm not going to stop now. I'll find your artefact.
I came north just to do this again? Forget it.
That's the spirit! Getting there will be extremely difficult, however. You will need a good detector, to spot both the artefacts and the anomalies. Now go, the march of science moves with you!
I'll be back soon.
Wait, do you hear that? The Zone is calling out to us, I can feel it... Go on and take her gift. I will wait in Zaton for your return.
Thank you.
Professor... What is this? I've never seen an artefact like this before. Even just holding it, it feels strange.

So it's real then... To think, we nearly mistook it for an error in our data. Thank you, stalker. Our team, and the scientific community as a whole, owes you an incredible debt. Quickly, give me the artefact. Afterwards, we can talk about your reward.
If this helps uncover the mysteries of the Zone, it will be worth it. Here's your artefact.
On second thought, I did all the work. I deserve this power.
Don't you dare, stalker, we had a deal! Give me the artefact. Be reasonable.
Okay, okay, I was joking, calm down. Here's the artefact.
What are you going to do? You can't hurt me, not when I have the artefact's power. Now get out of my way.
This is my life's work! I won't let you leave with it - over my dead body!

Our paths may diverge here, stalker, but our team will never forget you. The contribution you have made to science is greater than I can express, but hopefully this reward goes some way to show you how much we value your help. Please take it, with the thanks of our entire research group. I'll be departing shortly to head back outside the Zone, but if our paths ever cross again, I will help you in any way I can. Thank you again, and farewell.
Professor Skabinsky, right? I've finally found you. How is your research going?

Are you the stalker who helped our research group? I'm sorry to say that your help was in vain. Our calculations were wrong, this damn place is not the center of the Zone after all. It was all for nothing.
Is there nothing more to be done?
You've already done everything you could. Just leave us now. At least you got something out of our failure.
Reporting in, sir. Do you have any special assignments for me?
You ready for some real work, soldier? I hope you're ready - this comes right from the top. I've got a list of priority targets for investigation that comes straight from Mission Command.
Retrieve documents from the labs under Agroprom

Intel reports that there are highly classified documents located in the underground complex beneath the Agroprom Research Institute. They contain important information regarding the experiments that were previously conducted here. Your mission is simple: retrieve those documents. I don't care how you do it. Are you ready?
Yes, sir. I'm on the case.
I'm not ready to take the job yet, sir.
Retrieve documents from the X-18 lab complex

This lab contains vital information about the development of the Caymanov Experimental Emitter. Your objective is to retrieve any documentation you can find regarding this research and bring it here to headquarters. You'll be handsomely rewarded for your work.
That's dangerous work, Colonel, but I'm ready for the challenge. I'll be back with those documents, sir.
Respectfully, I think I'll pass. Sir. I don't want to end up as a mutant's lunch.
Get the documents from the X-16 lab

We need the research notes detailing the work that was done in that lab. I won't lie to you, this is going to be a tough one - you're going to have to survive deadly radiation and psi-fields to find these documents. Intel is sparse at best, but we can say for sure that you'll have to fight through a small army of zombies and mutants. I reccomend that you find the mobile laboratory in Yantar and speak to the science team posted there - they should be able to make something that'll shield you from the psi-fields surrounding the complex. So? Are you ready?
It's as good as done, sir. I'll begin right away.
Apologies, sir - I don't have the training or the equipment for an operation like this. I can't take it just yet.
Get the documents from the X-19 lab

Your job is simple: break through the Radar, assault the complex which houses the so-called "Brain Scorcher," and shut it down while grabbing any documents you can find. The Monolith won't take this lying down - you're going to face heavy resistance from those zealots. Are you ready to head north?
I'm ready to assault the Scorcher, sir.
Sir, I still need to make some preparations. I am not ready to assault the Scorcher yet.
Get the documents from the X-8 lab

On the outskirts of Pripyat, there's a top-secret laboratory codenamed X-8. Your mission: retrieve any and all documentation of the research that was carried out there. You'll find the laboratory underneath the Jubilee building. Are you ready for your assignment?
Roger, sir, ready and waiting.
Not yet, sir. I need time to prepare.
Obtain documents from the Pripyat Underground.

Intelligence reports that there's a huge complex underneath Pripyat - a "city within a city," so to speak. Headquarters needs you to find out what experiments and research was carried out down there, and investigate the secret "Pripyat-1 Pipeline."
Understood, sir.
I'm not ready yet - I need to prepare for this mission.
Destroy the Monolith control room and retrieve the documents.
I've changed my mind.

We've made many attempts to storm Chernobyl itself and seize the damn control room - but every single one of those missions has been an abject failure. That doesn't change the fact that the control room is still a high-priority target. Your mission: destroy the control room and get out with all the intel you can find. Before you go, find a hermit in the Everglades - there's a Swamp Doctor who has information about the decoder needed to break the door that leads to the control room. And, ah, be careful, soldier. Nobody's manage to accomplish anything like this before. Come back in one piece, understood?
Of course, sir. I'm not planning on dying now after coming this far.
I don't have the gear or the experience to manage such a feat... I need to prepare myself.
Mission accomplished, Comrade Colonel! Here are the documents from the underground research institute.
Excellent work. You executed the mission cleanly and you're still in one piece - here's your well-deserved reward!
Mission accomplished, sir! I got the necessary documents from X-18.
Well done, soldier! Command is very interested in this information - and your performance hasn't gone unnoticed. A hefty, well-deserved reward for some fine work.
Grettings, Comrade Colonel! I've got the documents from X-16 in my hands and the psi-field surrounding Yantar has disabled.

Well done! That was one hell of a job! With the psi-fields down, we should be able to send in our special operations teams to secure the entire complex and ensure the scientists can carry out their work in relative safety. Here, your reward. You've more than earned it, soldier.
The operation was a success, sir. The "Brain Scorcher" is disabled, and here are the documents I managed to find in X-10.

The road to the North is now open again...we might actually have a chance to properly secure the Zone now. And it's all thanks to you. Hell, you might be one of the best fighters I've ever seen. Take this as a reward:
I'm back from Pripyat, sir, and I found the lab under the former KBO "Jubilee." Here are the documents from the lab itself.
Great work! With these documents, we'll be able to shed light on many of the mysteries of the Zone. Command will be thrilled with your work - and no, I haven't forgotten your reward:
Mission accomplished, commander! Here are the documents I retrieved from the Pripyat-1 Pipeline.

You completed your mission successfully...incredible. I'm amazed. You know, the soldiers posted here have started to see you as an inspiration - they see you blasting your way through the Zone and think to themselves that maybe they, too, do the same. Me? I look at you, soldier, and all I can think of is how a general's epaulettes would suit you better. Here. Take it. It's not a promotion to General, heh, but I'm sure an increased salary can't hurt.

I'm back from Chernobyl, sir. The system which controlled the Monolith has been destroyed - it turns out the Sarcophagus was an illusion being projected from the control bunker. Here are the documents, sir - it describes everything in detail.

It can't be - holy shit. Honestly, I was thinking I'd just sent my best man to his death - I'm fucking speechless. Just...okay. I'll radio Command immediately and let them know we - you - dealt the Monolith a crushing blow, and inform them of all the intel you secured. As for when the brass will decide when the mission to properly seize the Centre, well...that's up for them to decide. But thanks to you, for the first time, the Zone is within our grasp! We can, finally, take control of the Zone, cleanse it of mutants and give the boot to all the stalkers skulking around this place. You have my most sincere thanks and I can't congragulate you enough. I'm not bullshitting you - you're a goddamn hero, you understand? It's not just me, either - High Command believed in your ability to accomplish this mission, and accordingly set aside a reward and accompanying honours for you. Take it! They're yours by right!
Greetings. I'm here to help - what can I do?

Nice to finally see a friendly face around here for once - I hope the battle at the nuclear plant went well? I'm Sergeant Lucky, but you can just call me Vasil. I'll get right to the point - the Monolith forces are camped out in a base southeast of this, uh, glowing thing smack-dab in the middle of the forest. They've mined the approach, and posted snipers on the watchtowers, so be careful. We're moving out - try to get those fuckers before they see us, got it?
Got it. Come on, sergeant, I'll cover you.
Comrade Captain, I'm ready to take part in the assault.

All right. These bastards are holding three roadblocks: two of them on the approaches to the sarcophagus, one right in front of Unit 4. Two of our squads will launch an assault from both sides. Your job is to support our guys with fire - and shoot as many of those damn Monoliths as possible.
Roger that! I'm going in.
You know where we can get some tools around here?
I heard something, just give me some money, like 2,000.
Here you go.
I'll come back later.
I know, and for 4,000 I'll tell you.
Here, take it.
Thank you, I don't need it.
But you pay me some money, like 1000, then I'll tell you.
Here's your money.
I changed my mind.
2,500 and no less. Then we'll talk.
Here.
Money's tight.
Listen here, friend:
[furthermore...]
I don't know.
Look in the hiding places.
Leave me alone, I can't deal with you right now.
You should look in the Dark Valley at the bandit base. They don't have much tech, so you can borrow an extra set. But where exactly - I was not outlined.
At the dump look for the guys recently told me that they have something like a base in the local hangar. Rather, for rough work there and will be found.
Cordon, ATP. Wanted there gangsters extensive base zabahat, even a technician with the equipment brought, but he disappeared somewhere, but his stuff they say remained.

At the cordon, within the Southern checkpoint look for. I hope you know how to work with a map? Dangerous there, no one goes there, so it is possible that the tools are there for fine work. And there are some other trustworthy reasons for that.

In Agroprom, where the complex is central. There's also an anomaly with electrons and psi, you know? It's somewhere in that complex that's worth looking for. There is no time for people to gather all sorts of junk now, the point is strategic and the fight for it is constantly going on, but to see - saw all sorts of technical junk.

The cemetery of technology. That's right now go out of the bar and look for a car with the number "9941KIL", it will lead you to the cherished box. I heard from a Debtor, they were on a raid there, on reconnaissance in general. Well, they combed the area, and there snork - dark. Well, they conducted a "tactical retreat", hehe. One here is running away and noticed the cherished machine.

You to Agroprom, where the main military base. Too cool? And what can you do, but they are on the state support and technology they have "fat", not like ours, from a turd at least build a tank. You didn't think you could get calibration tools so easily, did you?
Thanks for the money, now piss off, I don't know where your tools are, you fucking crank.
No, I'm sorry, man, I forgot that place. Look in the stash houses, ask around the dealers.
Do you have any special assignments for me?

Nothing special yet, no - but I do have a job for you. We set up a scanning device in the archianomaly - the one south of the Western watchtower. By now it'll have collected all of the anomalous activity measurements we were looking for - now I just need someone to bring it back here. You up for it?
Some other time, maybe.
I'll do it.
I'm back, and I have the device. Here you go.
Thank you. By the way, there's a scout coming back to camp tonight, some time between eight to eleven PM. Stick around - we'll meet with him.
Is there anything I can do for Clear Sky?

Yeah, there is. You know, we've regained some control over our little corner of the Swamps, but even so our manpower leaves a lot to be desired. Anomalous activity is down; normally, that'd be a good thing, but instead the Swamps are crawling with Bandits and Renegades looking for easy work. Thanks to those shitheads, we're losing territory, giving up position after position. We're boxed in and our men are exhausted - those Renegades in particular never let up. Last I heard, those bastards are prepping for another assault on our outpost at the Pumping Station - can you help our guys fight them off?
I can't help you out with that right now - another time.
No problem. I'll take care of it.
The Renegades have been destroyed.

All right! Next up, we need to secure the road to the Cordon. I've already prepared an assault team to take the South Village; move into position and help them out. You can go with them if you'd like - the guys are just about ready to head out of the base.
The road to the Cordon is secure.

Fantastic work. Thanks to you, we've secured all of the key positions in the Swamp. Here's your reward. Still, it's not all good news - this time, it's the military that's the issue. Previously, the Renegades and whatever other trash came with them wouldn't dare push deep into our territory since they weren't willing to pick a fight with the soldiers in the area; we weren't exactly friends with the military, but they were content with leaving us alone so long as we didn't bother them in turn. Now? The military's been sending more and more squads of their own stalkers into our turf, and we're taking heavy losses. I've had enough. Kick them out of the machine yard in the northeast of the Swamps. That'll finally give us the advantage here, and ensure Clear Sky can finally gain a strong, secure foothold in the area. I'm sending an assault team composed of our most experienced fighters - they're already heading out from our base. Join up with them and help them win this fight.
The military has been knocked out of the machine yard - it was a tough fight.

Incredible! Thanks to your help, we've retaken all the key positions we'd lost and are once again a force to be reckoned with. Ahh, if only Lebedev was here to see this...ahem. Enough of the sentimentality. We've got a lot of work to do now that we're not in crisis mode - here's your reward. And again, thank you for your help. We couldn't have done this without you.
The scout is dead. Here's his PDA.
A damn shame about that - he was one of our best. Thanks.
I'm here about the decrypted PDA.

Here, take a seat. I've got some good news for you. It turns out that some of our comrades who went missing back in 2011 actually survived! According to the information we took off the PDA, a mercenary who went by the name of Scar might still be around. Back in 2011, he was leading an assault team through Limansk and the Hospital into the CNPP in order to catch up with Strelok and stop him; according to our scout, he had good intel that someone who fits Scar's description was still kicking, and he was even seen in contact with some stalkers who might be survivors from Lebedev's group. The best course of action, if you ask me, would be to head straight over to Sidorovich and see if he knows anything about all this.
Whoa, that's fantastic news. And yeah, you're right - if anyone would know anything about all this, it'd be Sidorovich. I'll ask him and see if I can't find out anything about our surviving comrades.
I found one surviving member of Clear Sky.
You found him? Who is he? Where is he?

It's Gamayun, from Lebedev's old group. He's currently fighting with the Outcasts of the Monolith. I couldn't convince him to return to the Swamps with me, though. He said that he couldn't leave his brothers behind.

Well, I suppose I trust his judgement - and besides, if he survived, perhaps there are others who made it out alive too. Your story gives me hope that one day, we will be reunited with our old comrades. I'll have our intelligence units provide assistance to the Outcasts. Oh, and I haven't forgotten your part in all this. You deserve a reward for your work.
Thank you.(chuckles)
Hey! Are you Scar? I need to speak with you.
Yeah, that's me. Who the hell are you? And what do you - shit, not now. Help me fight off these pseudo-dogs first! They've been chasing me for two kilometres.
Sure. We can fight them off together.
Phew, we did it. So? Can you tell me about the surviving members of Lebedev's group? I have it on good authority that you know something about all this.

Yeah, you're right, I do know something. I'm afraid I can't tell you all that much, though - most of my knowledge isn't much more than rumours. Have you heard about the stalker known as Strider and the crew he runs with? Apparently they're all former memebers of the Monolith, but they rebelled against their masters and these days are engaged in a guerilla campaign against their former comrades. Rumour has it that these "Outcasts" count former members of Clear Sky amongst their ranks. You'll find Strider and his unit in Jupiter close to the former army checkpoint - used to be the stomping ground of a bunch of Bandits led by a guy named Valet, 'til he took one in the dome, anyway.
Thank you very much.
I found Gamayun's crew, and he told me to bring you this device. The squad did their job.
Well, this device will turn the tide in our favour. I'm glad you found him, even if it's a shame he didn't come with you. He's one hell of a fighter.
He's decided to stay with the Outcasts.
Good. I think you deserve a reward for helping our group. We're not rich, but we have enough to pay you for a job well done. There you go.
Thank you.
I hear you have some former members of Clear Sky in your ranks - can I speak with them? I work for Clear Sky and we're looking for our comrades.
Hrm. We do have some guys who used to be with Clear Sky - they were Monolith soldiers like the rest of us, until the C-Conciousness loosened its grip. Now we stand together against the Monolith.
So where can I meet them?

I'm afraid you're too late. Their unit's gone on a mission to eastern Pripyat and unfortunately, they haven't reported in yet. If you hurry, you might be able to catch up with them. I don't have many men, so I can't spare anyone for a rescue mission.
Thank you for the information. I'll be sure to find them and if they're in danger I'll give them a hand.
Looks like I got here just in time.
Yeah, well...those fanatics would've killed us if we'd been any closer. Did Strider send you?
I'm actually looking for you. You're a member of Lebedev's group, right? Clear Sky is looking for you.

Hmm...I used to be. But now I'm an Outcast of the Monolith. I'll tell you everything I know and we'll decide what to do next, but for now I need you to help carry out my mission while you're here. The Monolith forces we're up against are tougher than we thought. I've sent a unit to the river port to seize a device that can be used to listen in on those fanatics, but they haven't responded to any of my messages. Seems like the Monolith captured them. I need you to recon the territory and help us get this device back.
All right, I'll help.
Here's the device.
Thank you. You'd better get this back to Strider. I'll stay here, and we'll secure this outpost with my boys.
So you're not coming to the Swamp with me?

No. I'm sorry, but I'm an Outcast. I can't leave my brothers behind. Oh, I promised you I'd tell you my story, didn't I? Listen up. Back in 2011, I was with Lebedev as part of an assault team charged with breaking through Limansk. With the help of some mercenaries we pushed further north, and we managed to get a foothold within the city limits - then, there was that huge emission. After that? I don't remember anything - Strider says I served the Monolith like everyone else that got caught in the emission. The next thing I remember was waking up in Jupiter some time in 2012 as myself. I only survived thanks to Strider, and since then I've been working with him.
Thank you for telling me your story. I'll pass the device on to Strider, and I'll make sure to say hello to Cold for you.
Is there a special job?

Come to think of it, yes. There's a war going on with the Freedom and the bandits, plus there's fanatics coming from the north. All our forces are at war, and we need hunters for our main task - to destroy the Zone. I'm offering you a job as a witch doctor. It's as simple as that: I offer you a "witch" contract, and you fulfill it and get a reward for it. How's that sound?
Sounds good. If the pay is decent, I'll take it. What's my first contract?

Don't worry about the pay, we'll pay you in money and good equipment if you prove yourself. Your first contract will be to destroy a den of bloodsuckers that have migrated south. It's a pretty big pain in the ass, the bloodsuckers have gotten past all our posts and patrols. Destroy these critters and come back for your reward. I suggest you start your search for the lair with the Butcher, he's the leader of the local hunters.
I'll take that contract. Consider these bloodsuckers already dead.
Mission accomplished, bloodsuckers destroyed.
Good job, here's your reward.
So, what do I have to do to get information on Scar?

Well, here's the thing... A box with a report from one of the expeditions that went north was handed over to the roadblock from deep in the Zone. During the day they guard it as if it were filled with pure plutonium, but at night for some reason leave no more than two guards. Sneak up to the roadblock and get that box for me. And I'll spare no expense to you... within reason. You'll do it, won't you ?
I'll do it.
You know, I don't think so. I value my life.
Well, I've got the money ready, but don't... don't make any noise, okay? We'd all be in for a world of hurt if the military gets pissed off.
I found them, here.
Good, very good my friend. Here's a small payment to cover your travel expenses and your bullets eheheh. Now let's talk business to get these information you are after...
I'm looking for a certain mercenary named Scar. You know him?
Maybe I do. Why do you need this info?
He knows information that's important to Clear Sky.

Huh. Yeah, I can sell you some information, but it'll cost you. Let's say...twenty-thousand, and you owe me a favour as well. Do a job for me to prove that I can trust you - then we'll see what I can tell you about Scar.
Okay, I'll run one of your errands and buy the information.
I don't have time for these games, you greasy fuck.
And I don't have time for broke, lazy assholes. If you're not here to do business then get the fuck outta here.
I'm here for the intel you have on Scar.
You got the money? Did you finish my assignment?
Not yet. I'll be back soon.
Yeah. Here's the money, and the job's done, too.

Perfect. Listen close - or better yet, write it down. I'm not telling you twice, heh. So, this merc came to see me - he needed gear for a deep raid into the northern territories. The man had deep pockets, lemme tell you - spent a ton of cash on top-quality gear. From what I could tell, he was looking for something in Zaton - I'm sure that's where he is now. I've also heard he's holed up in the substation workshops, searching for something in all that junk. That's all you get. Good luck finding him.
Good doing business with you. Thanks for the info.
